hero.headline: Fresh From the Farm — Delivered to You
hero.subtitle: Connect directly with farmers across Uganda, Kenya, Tanzania, and Rwanda. From the smallest village garden to large plantations — ShambaNi brings the market to every farmer.

categories.title: Fresh Produce Available Now
categories.subtitle: Direct from Farmers Across East Africa

howItWorks.title: Simple. Fast. Fair.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Any farmer can list via web or simply dial *220# on any phone — no smartphone needed. List your crops in seconds, set your price, and reach thousands of buyers.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Schools, businesses, and households browse fresh produce and place orders directly with farmers. Filter by district, category, or price to find exactly what you need.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Farmer coordinates delivery. Pay securely via Airtel Money, MTN Mobile Money, PayPal, or Bank Transfer. Just 2.5% platform fee — the rest goes straight to the farmer.

ussd.title: No Smartphone? No Problem!
ussd.description: Dial *220# on any phone to list your produce, check your orders, and get paid — even on a basic feature phone.
ussd.feature2: Get SMS alerts when orders come in
ussd.feature4: Available in English, Luganda, Kiswahili & Runyarwanda

crossBorder.title: From Uganda to East Africa
crossBorder.description: ShambaNi started in Uganda, but our vision spans all of East Africa. We are building connections between farmers and buyers across four nations, breaking down barriers with technology that works on any phone.

payments.title: Pay Your Way — Administered Securely
payments.airtelDesc: Fast mobile money transfers across East Africa. Available in Uganda, Kenya, Tanzania, and Rwanda.
payments.mtnDesc: Uganda's largest mobile money network. Instant transfers, no bank account needed.
payments.paypalDesc: International payments powered by PayPal. Handle: @LutwamaReagan. Secure buyer protection included.
payments.bankDesc: Direct bank transfers for large orders. All major East African banks supported.
payments.feeNotice: Just 2.5% platform fee — the lowest in the region. The rest goes directly to our farmers.

testimonials.title: Real Stories from Farmers and Buyers
testimonials.quote1: Before ShambaNi I used to throw away excess cabbage. Now I sell everything! Last month I earned UGX 180,000 from my small garden. This platform changed my life.
testimonials.quote2: We buy tomatoes and matooke directly from farmers. Fresher produce, cheaper prices, and we know exactly where it comes from. Our students eat better because of ShambaNi.
testimonials.quote3: I don't have a smartphone but I dial *220# to list my groundnuts. I have received 3 orders already this week! Even without internet, I can sell my produce.

cta.title: Ready to Join East Africa's Farm Marketplace?
cta.subtitle: Whether you're a farmer looking to sell or a buyer seeking fresh produce — ShambaNi connects you.

footer.tagline: Empowering East Africa's farmers — from the smallest garden to the largest plantation.

product.head: head
product.deliveryInfo: Delivery available within the district. Contact farmer to arrange.
product.farmerBio: A dedicated farmer with years of experience growing quality produce. Committed to sustainable farming practices and delivering fresh, healthy products to customers across East Africa.

farmerRegister.title: Register Your Farm
farmerRegister.pageSubtitle: Start selling your produce to thousands of buyers across East Africa. Registration is free and takes just a few minutes.
farmerRegister.step3Desc: How would you like to receive payments?
farmerRegister.phoneNote: This number will be used for USSD access and SMS alerts
farmerRegister.languageNote: This will be your default USSD language
farmerRegister.farmAboutPlaceholder: Describe your farm, what you grow/raise, your experience...
farmerRegister.paymentInfoNote: All payments are administered by ShambaNi. Funds are held securely until delivery is confirmed, then released to you minus the 2.5% platform fee.
farmerRegister.submitNote: Your application will be reviewed within 24-48 hours.
farmerRegister.successMessage: Thank you for joining ShambaNi. Our team will review your application and contact you within 24-48 hours. In the meantime, you can start listing your produce by dialing *220# on any phone.

admin.feeDescription: This fee is deducted from each transaction. The farmer receives the remaining amount.
admin.paypalDescription: The PayPal handle used for receiving international payments.

admin.languageDesc: Set the default language for the platform.
admin.countriesDesc: Select which countries are active on the platform.

admin.emailNotifications: Send email notifications to admin on pending approvals

ussdPage.heroDesc: You don't need a smartphone or internet connection to sell on ShambaNi. Just dial a simple code on your basic phone and start reaching buyers across East Africa.
ussdPage.step1Desc: Open your phone's dialer and enter *220#. Press call. No app download needed — this works on any phone, even the oldest Nokia.
ussdPage.step2Desc: A simple menu appears on your screen. Choose what you want to do: list produce, check orders, or see your payments. Use number keys to navigate.
ussdPage.step3Desc: When a buyer places an order, you get an SMS alert. Deliver the produce, and payment is sent directly to your Airtel Money, MTN Mobile Money, or bank account.
ussdPage.smsMockup: New order! Buyer wants 5 trays of eggs. Total: UGX 57,500. Reply CONFIRM to accept.
ussdPage.exploreDesc: Tap through the menu options below to see what you can do with USSD.
ussdPage.langDesc: ShambaNi USSD works in 4 East African languages. Switch anytime during your session.
ussdPage.sms1Content: New order received! John M. wants 10kg of tomatoes from your farm. Reply CONFIRM to accept or CANCEL to decline. — ShambaNi
ussdPage.sms2Content: Payment of UGX 35,000 for order #1234 has been sent to your Airtel Money. Thank you for using ShambaNi!
ussdPage.sms3Content: This week's prices: Tomatoes UGX 3,500/kg (↑5%), Eggs UGX 11,500/tray (→), Matooke UGX 15,000/bunch (↓3%). List your produce now!
ussdPage.faqQ1: Does USSD cost money to use?
ussdPage.faqA1: Standard USSD rates from your mobile network apply (usually free or a few shillings per session). ShambaNi does not charge any additional fees for using *220#.
ussdPage.faqA2: Any mobile phone with a dialer works — from the oldest Nokia to the latest iPhone. No internet, no app, no smartphone required. If you can make a call, you can use ShambaNi.
ussdPage.faqQ3: How do I receive payments without a bank account?
ussdPage.faqA3: You can receive payments via Airtel Money or MTN Mobile Money directly to your phone. No bank account needed. The money arrives as mobile money on your phone.
ussdPage.faqQ4: Can I change my listed prices via USSD?
ussdPage.faqA4: Yes! Dial *220#, select 'List Produce', then 'Update Prices'. You can change prices anytime.
ussdPage.faqQ5: How will I know when someone orders my produce?
ussdPage.faqA5: You will receive an SMS message instantly when a buyer places an order. The SMS includes the buyer's details, what they want, and the total amount.
ussdPage.faqA6: Absolutely. We protect all farmer data with encryption. Your phone number and payment details are never shared with buyers. All transactions are processed securely by our admin team.
ussdPage.ctaDesc: Dial *220# now or register online to reach thousands of buyers across East Africa.

aboutPage.heroTitle: Empowering Every Farmer in East Africa
aboutPage.heroDesc: ShambaNi exists to bridge the gap between rural farmers and urban markets. We believe that every farmer — whether they own a smartphone or a basic feature phone — deserves access to a thriving marketplace.
aboutPage.storyPara1: It started with a simple observation: millions of farmers across East Africa grow incredible produce, yet many struggle to find buyers. Meanwhile, urban consumers, schools, and restaurants search endlessly for fresh, affordable, locally-grown food. The disconnect isn't about quality — it's about connection.
aboutPage.storyPara2: ShambaNi was built to be that connection. But we knew that building a website wasn't enough. Most smallholder farmers don't have smartphones — they have basic feature phones that can only make calls and send texts. So we built a USSD system (*220#) that lets any farmer list produce, check orders, and get paid using nothing more than a dial pad.
aboutPage.storyPara3: Today, ShambaNi operates across Uganda, Kenya, Tanzania, and Rwanda — in over 200 districts, in 4 languages, connecting farmers to buyers through the web, mobile, and USSD. Our platform fee is just 2.5%, among the lowest in the region, because we believe the money should go to those who grow the food.
aboutPage.pullQuote: Technology should adapt to the farmer. Not the other way around.
aboutPage.why1Desc: Our USSD system (*220#) lets farmers without smartphones list produce, check orders, and get paid. No internet, no app, no barrier.
aboutPage.why2Desc: One of the lowest fees in the region. We believe farmers should keep what they earn. The rest goes straight to growing their farm.
aboutPage.why3Desc: Operating in 4 countries with local language support. We're building a regional marketplace that connects rural production to urban demand.
aboutPage.paymentsDesc: Secure payments administered by our team. Multiple options for every transaction.
aboutPage.hiw1Desc: Any farmer can list via web or simply dial *220# on any phone — no smartphone needed.
aboutPage.hiw2Desc: Schools, businesses, and households browse fresh produce and place orders directly with farmers.
aboutPage.hiw3Desc: Farmer coordinates delivery. Pay securely via Airtel Money, MTN Mobile Money, PayPal, or Bank Transfer.
aboutPage.visionText: By 2028, ShambaNi aims to connect 10,000 farmers across East Africa, process UGX 5 billion in transactions, and expand to all major agricultural districts in the region. We envision a future where no farmer is left behind because of the phone they own — where USSD, web, and mobile work together to create the most inclusive agricultural marketplace in Africa.
aboutPage.founderQuote: I built ShambaNi because I saw my own family struggle to sell their produce. My uncle would travel for hours to find a buyer for his bananas, often returning with nothing. Technology should solve this, not make it harder. That's why USSD is at the heart of everything we do.
aboutPage.ctaDesc: Whether you're a farmer ready to sell or a buyer looking for the freshest produce — we're here to connect you.
